contents.value: **@import** — Less keyword.
**@media** — Less keyword.
**@font-face** — Less keyword.
**@keyframes** — Less keyword.
**@charset** — Less keyword.
**lighten** — Built-in function/command in Less.
**darken** — Built-in function/command in Less.
**saturate** — Built-in function/command in Less.
**desaturate** — Built-in function/command in Less.
**fadein** — Built-in function/command in Less.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

**Flexbox** is a CSS layout model for distributing space along a single axis.
```css
display: flex; justify-content: center;
```

**CSS Grid** is a two-dimensional layout system.
```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr;
```

A **pseudo-class** selects elements in a specific state.
```css
a:hover, input:focus, li:nth-child(2)
```

CSS **custom properties** (variables) store reusable values.
```css
:root { --color: blue; } .el { color: var(--color); }
```
**Specificity** determines which CSS rule applies when multiple rules target the same element. Inline > ID > Class > Tag.

```less
color(string) -> color
```
Parses a color string (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla, or named color keyword) and returns a color value that can be used in further color manipulation functions. This is the base function for constructing color values programmatically in Less stylesheets.

```less
mix(color1, color2[, weight]) -> color
```
Blends two colors together proportionally based on the weight parameter (defaulting to 50%). A weight of 25% mixes 25% of color1 with 75% of color2. Opacity is also factored into the weighting calculation for semi-transparent colors.

```less
fade(color, amount) -> color
```
Sets the absolute opacity of a color to the specified percentage amount (0% to 100%). Unlike fadein/fadeout which adjust opacity relatively, fade sets it to an exact value, making it predictable for creating semi-transparent overlay colors.

```less
spin(color, angle) -> color
```
Rotates the hue of a color by the given angle in degrees on the color wheel (0 to 360). Positive values rotate clockwise, negative counter-clockwise. Useful for generating complementary and analogous color schemes from a single base color.

```less
percentage(number) -> percentage
```
Converts a unitless number to a percentage value by multiplying by 100. For example, percentage(0.5) returns 50%. Useful when computing relative dimensions or widths from ratios stored in variables.

```less
unit(dimension[, unit]) -> number|dimension
```
Changes or removes the unit of a dimension value. With one argument, strips the unit and returns a unitless number. With two arguments, replaces the unit with the specified one. Commonly used for unit conversions and arithmetic between incompatible unit types.

```less
.mixin-name(@param1: default, @param2: default) { ... }
```
Defines a reusable set of CSS declarations (a mixin) that can accept parameters with optional default values. Mixins are invoked by their selector name and can be included in any ruleset. They support pattern matching, guards (when conditions), and the ... rest argument syntax for variadic parameters.

```less
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```less
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```less
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```less
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```less
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```less
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```less
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

```less
print(value)
```
Output a value to the standard output stream.

```less
length
```
Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
